creator: ohartmann@walstatt.org
text:
I did not say that __I__ reported, I did say, that there are reports (findings via google). I did report to this PR database! It might be the wrong place, but my thinking is that a maintainer has a closer touch to upstream as a commoner.

By the way, the documentation of net-snmp is crap and more like a unmaintained bunch of words. Or it is written in a confusing way - either way, calling snmpd or snmptrapd with the option -H (as I recall, it is definitely -H, but do not remember wether it is snmpd or snmptrapd) reveals several supported variables - but with none or sparse information of the syntax of the arguments.

Fact is that if one want to have logOption, he has to define this specific option in ${PREFIX}/etc/snmp/snmp.conf and it seems to me that snmptrapd is also reading this config file. I do not know exactly. The syntax of what has to be applied as attribute to this option is documented very weird. But when I setup the snmp.conf file with

logOption  syslog

starting snmptrapd then complains about a wrong syntax in "y" in exactly that option. It revealed itself after hours of studying the documentation, that the syntax is as the option "-L" implies - and that is also exactly that what is the headline of the option "logoption" in snmptrapd.conf when this specific files is created via snmpconf (there is a rough hint, but as scientists already know, several aspects of explanations are better to catch with a suitable example).

As long this weird behaviour is not due to some FreeBSD specific mistakes in the config, I guess it is better to close this PR.

By the way - I tried to close this PR with a suitable flag. Why isn't there a flag like "Up to upstream" or "UNFIXABLE"?
